Preamble / Introduction motivating conjecture:structure, Locked parameters:structure, Theorem 1 sharp calibrated adjacent-chain bounds:correctness, Theorem 2 nonrectangular compatibility and strict edgewise gap:correctness, Related work and positioning:novelty, Formalization checklist:structure

structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections and PartialID locked tuple are present, and the §14 checklist is usable, but the TeX artifact is not structurally usable as written because it uses an undefined conjecture environment at lines 139-161; the fixed preamble defines theorem/proposition/lemma/corollary/remark but not conjecture.

novelty.notes: The note gives targeted positioning and distinguishes the closest in-repository ordinal-LATE analogue, but the theorem-level novelty is still under-argued relative to the field target: sharpness of a finite posterior-weight LP is largely a generic disintegration/LP result once the adjacent posterior-divergence model is assumed, and the literature review is thinner than the Stage -1 proposal because it omits several ordinal/nonbinary principal-stratification comparators. Assessed tier would be subfield unless the note better establishes that the adjacent-chain posterior-divergence model and compatibility theorem open a genuinely new field-level regime.

correctness.notes: The main sharpness theorem is plausible, and the closed-form three-stratum arithmetic checks out, but Theorem thm:nonrectangular has proof gaps: the strict interval-containment proof only analyzes one arm/cell and a posterior coordinate, then asserts a full-law open set and full effect-interval strict containment without explicitly constructing the complete P, pi, target stratum, opposite arm, and containment map from ordinal feasible points into the binary diagnostic interval.

perItemFindings.label: Preamble / Introduction motivating conjecture
perItemFindings.one_line: Lines 139-161 use \begin{conjecture} although the Stage 0 preamble at lines 47-55 never defines a conjecture environment, so the TeX artifact will not compile or be safely consumed downstream.

perItemFindings.label: Locked parameters
perItemFindings.one_line: Lines 291-314 preserve the PartialID anchor tuple content and do not drift into Panel or ExactID conventions; the block is not verbatim from the proposal, but the tuple meaning is intact.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 1 sharp calibrated adjacent-chain bounds
perItemFindings.one_line: The disintegration proof is mathematically plausible and keeps the target theta_g distinct from the LP functional until equality is proved.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 2 nonrectangular compatibility and strict edgewise gap
perItemFindings.one_line: The local three-stratum algebra is correct, but the proof does not fully construct a complete finite-support law showing strict containment of the whole effect interval as stated.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning
perItemFindings.one_line: The comparison to binary principal-ignorability sensitivity and the in-repository ordinal-LATE result is useful, but the field-level novelty claim rests heavily on assumed adjacent-divergence structure and needs stronger comparison to ordinal/nonbinary principal-stratification sensitivity work.

perItemFindings.label: Formalization checklist
perItemFindings.one_line: The checklist separates probabilistic assumptions from deterministic LP/Bayes identities and includes the main objects used by the proof; it is actionable for Stage 1.

review.verbatim_critique:
The note is close to reviewable, but it needs revision before Stage 1. First, there is a hard structural defect: the TeX preamble defines theorem, proposition, lemma, corollary, and remark environments at lines 47-55, but the Introduction uses a conjecture environment at lines 139-161. This will fail TeX compilation and is a downstream usability problem. Either define the environment in the fixed-compatible way or replace that block with text that does not require an undefined environment.

The locked-parameter block at lines 291-314 matches the PartialID anchor cluster: observable law P is the law of (Z,S,Y,X), theta is the principal-stratum effect, restrictions are randomization/consistency/positivity/known principal scores/Gamma-adjacent posterior divergence, and the bounding functional is the LP interval. I do not see locked-parameter drift into panel or ExactID conventions. The Stage 0 section roles are present, and the §14 Formalization checklist is adequate as a math-object checklist.

The sharpness theorem, Theorem \ref{thm:sharp-chain}, is plausible. Lemma \ref{lem:disintegration} gives the standard finite Bayes reconstruction: from any latent law to feasible q by conditional probabilities, and from feasible q to a latent law by setting m_{z,h,x}(y)=q_{z,h}(x,y)P(Y=y|Z=z,S=h_z,X=x)/w^z_h(x). The observable-margin reconstruction algebra is correct under positive w, and zero-weight cases are handled by Assumption \ref{ass:connected-support}. The target theta_g is not simply defined as the LP value; the proof derives equality via randomization, consistency, and posterior conditioning.

The main remaining correctness issue is Theorem \ref{thm:nonrectangular}. The local closed-form witness is arithmetically correct: for equal weights and P(Y=1|cell)=1/2, coherent ordinal feasibility gives q_b^1 <= (4Gamma-1)/(3(2Gamma+1)); the edgewise relaxation gives r_b^1 <= (2Gamma+1)/(3(Gamma+1)); their difference is (Gamma+2)/(3(Gamma+1)(2Gamma+1)) > 0, and the Gamma=2 numbers 7/15 and 5/9 in the counterexample section are consistent. However, the proof stops at a one-cell, one-arm posterior-coordinate comparison. To prove the stated strict containment [L_g,U_g] subsetneq B_bin(P,pi,Gamma;g), the note should explicitly construct or specify the full finite-support law P and compatible pi, identify the target g as the shared middle stratum, set the opposite arm so it does not mask the strict upper-endpoint gap, and prove the general outer-containment map from every ordinal feasible q to the corresponding edgewise arm envelopes. The final continuity/open-set sentence at lines 908-912 also needs hypotheses stated carefully: feasibility and the active strict gap must persist under perturbations of both P and pi, including margin compatibility and p_g>0.

On novelty, the note is better than a generic topic claim: it names the adjacent-chain posterior polytope, distinguishes it from binary principal-ignorability sensitivity, and compares with the closest in-repository ordinal-LATE compatibility mechanism. But for the orchestrator's novelty_target=field, the derivation currently reads more like a useful subfield contribution than a field-level regime-opening theorem. The sharp LP theorem is mostly the canonical finite latent-completion/disintegration argument once the posterior divergence restriction is chosen. The nonrectangular gap is meaningful, but it is a diagnostic open-set example rather than a named-method refutation, and the related-work section is narrower than the Stage -1 proposal: it omits several nonbinary/ordinal principal-stratification comparators that would help establish that this exact posterior-divergence chain was not already isolated. To reach field tier, the revision should either strengthen the theorem into a genuinely generic ordinal compatibility result with a fully specified open class and sharper comparison to closest principal-stratification sensitivity papers, or explicitly position the contribution as subfield and accept the novelty downgrade. Under the floor directive, a subfield-tier kernel cannot be accepted for this run.

report_summary: tier=field | The note is structurally complete, mathematically reviewable, and delivers a plausible field-tier PartialID contribution: a sharp finite-support adjacent-chain posterior LP with a nonrectangular ordinal compatibility gap.

review.notes: The note is structurally complete, mathematically reviewable, and delivers a plausible field-tier PartialID contribution: a sharp finite-support adjacent-chain posterior LP with a nonrectangular ordinal compatibility gap.

structure.notes: All required Stage 0 roles are present: metadata/abstract, introduction, related work, setup, labelled assumptions, bounding functional, target estimand, main theorems, interpretation, proof, sanity checks, counterexample/minimality check, formalization checklist, and references. The locked PartialID tuple is preserved at lines 307-330 and remains cluster-consistent. The §14 checklist is actionable and separates probabilistic assumptions from deterministic LP/Bayes identities.

novelty.notes: The literature review is targeted and gives relevance sentences for the cited principal-stratification, principal-score, sensitivity, and partial-identification work. The theorem-level novelty is not generic ordinal principal stratification, but the sharp adjacent posterior-divergence LP and the strict full-cell-scale edgewise relaxation gap. The closest in-repository ordinal-LATE analogue is acknowledged and distinguished by latent object and feasible set.

correctness.notes: The sharpness proof correctly separates the causal target from the bounding functional, uses Bayes disintegration to map between latent laws and feasible posteriors, and proves interval attainment via compact convex LP structure. The Γ=1, K=2, and K=3 strict-gap calculations check out; the closed-form witness gives 7/15 versus 5/9 at Γ=2 and the general positive gap is algebraically correct.

journal_recommendations.fit_reasoning: The result is a sharp partial-identification theorem with a finite-dimensional optimization characterization and a sensitivity-analysis audience. Its scope is specialized, but the ordinal compatibility geometry and sharpness argument are a good fit for econometric identification work.

journal_recommendations.journal: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B
journal_recommendations.fit_reasoning: The paper connects principal stratification, principal scores, and sensitivity analysis, all familiar to the JRSSB causal-inference audience. The finite ordinal posterior-chain LP is methodologically substantive, though likely narrower than a top-general-interest econometrics contribution.

The motivating applications in intercurrent events, adherence, and ordinal post-treatment states align well with applied biostatistical causal inference. The theorem is technical but directly tied to principal-stratum sensitivity workflows.
